✦ project // case study

Custom PC Build

Building my own gaming and development workstation from scratch

category hardware
status complete
role Builder & System Administrator
timeline Research + Build completed over 2 months
tech stack:
AMD Ryzen NVIDIA RTX 5080 64GB DDR5 RAM 2 TB NVMe SSD Windows 11
Links coming soon
🖥️ TODO: Add hero screenshot

📋 project_overview.md

I built a custom PC from the ground up to serve as both a gaming rig and a development workstation. This project involved extensive research into component compatibility, thermal management, and performance optimization. The end result is a powerful future-proof system that handles everything from compiling large codebases to running modern games at high settings.

❓ the_problem.txt

Pre-built computers often come with compromises — either overpriced for the specs, poor airflow, or locked-down components that limit future upgrades. I wanted a system perfectly tailored to my needs: fast compile times for development, smooth gaming performance, and room to upgrade as technology evolves.

💡 the_solution.txt

I researched each component individually, reading benchmarks and reviews to find the best value for my budget. I chose a modular approach with a quality case, efficient cooling, and components that work well together. The build process taught me hands-on skills about cable management, thermal paste application, and BIOS configuration.

✨ features.list

Component Selection

Carefully researched each part for compatibility and value

Thermal Management

Optimized airflow with case fans and quality CPU cooler

Dual Boot Setup

Windows 11 for gaming, Linux for development work

Future-Proof Design

Chose components with upgrade paths for RAM, storage, and GPU

🖼️ gallery.screenshots

🖼️ TODO: Add gallery screenshots

🧩 challenges.log

challenge:

Component Compatibility

Ensuring all parts work together — motherboard chipset, RAM speed, power supply wattage

solution:

Used PCPartPicker and manufacturer specs to verify compatibility before purchasing

challenge:

Cable Management

First build had messy cables affecting airflow

solution:

Watched tutorials, used cable ties, and routed cables behind motherboard tray

challenge:

BIOS Configuration

Enabling XMP profiles and boot priorities was confusing at first

solution:

Read motherboard manual and followed community guides for optimal settings

🎯 outcomes.results

  • Achieved 30% faster compile times compared to previous laptop
  • Smooth 1440p gaming at high settings
  • Learned hands-on hardware skills applicable to future builds
  • Successfully set up dual-boot environment for development flexibility

🚀 next_steps.md

Future upgrades planned include adding more storage (another NVMe drive), upgrading to a newer GPU generation when prices normalize, and potentially custom water cooling for better thermals and aesthetics.
